title: Pattern breaking: a complex systems approach to psychedelic medicine.

assets.abstract: Recent research has demonstrated the potential of psychedelic therapy for mental health care. However, the psychological experience underlying its therapeutic effects remains poorly understood. This paper proposes a framework that suggests psychedelics act as destabilizers, both psychologically and neurophysiologically. Drawing on the 'entropic brain' hypothesis and the 'RElaxed Beliefs Under pSychedelics' model, this paper focuses on the richness of psychological experience. Through a complex s

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Nonordinary — psychedelic decoupling, ego dissolution, DMN disruption, altered states.
